message:
Landing headline: lead with the in-browser RPC numbers (now bigger)

Old headline still cited the in-process Node bench: "Burst 1000 calls,
2.5 µs, 3.2× faster". Real users won't run that — they'll click
Playground or RPC bench. The new in-browser /rpc.html shows wins
between 4-6× burst, 1.3-1.7× big-payload, and 25% less wire — bigger
than the old in-process claim AND verifiable on the page itself.

Reordered the rows so the wins users can click-through-and-verify
are at the top, the "tied on localhost" caveat is right below them,
and the two losses (bundle, cold start) stay at the bottom in the
honest column.

files.patch:
@@ -23,28 +23,28 @@

capnwasm

vs capnweb & REST/JSON

- In-browser fetch+render (any workload) - essentially tied — localhost network dominates -
-
- Burst 1000 RPC calls (in-process, per-call) - 2.5 μs — 3.2x faster than capnweb + RPC burst, 200 concurrent calls (browser→Node WS) + ~4–6× faster than capnweb
- 64 KB text echo round-trip (in-process) - 96 μs — 3.8x faster than capnweb + RPC 64 KB binary echo (browser→Node WS) + ~1.3–1.7× faster, 25% less wire
Wire bytes for 4 KB binary blob (gzip) 2.4× smaller than JSON / capnweb
+
+ In-browser parallel HTTP fetch (small records) + essentially tied — localhost network dominates +
Bundle (JS + wasm, gzip) - 44 KB — 2.1x larger than capnweb + 44 KB — 2.1× larger than capnweb
Cold start (init + first call) - ~3 ms — 15x slower than capnweb + ~3 ms — 15× slower than capnweb
